qas.q:
2.2 Director, National Security Agency (DIRNSA)
DIRNSA is designated as
qas.a: The National Manager for National Security Telecommunications and Information Systems Security

qas.q:
2.2 Director, National Security Agency (DIRNSA)
DIRNSA is responsible to
qas.a: The Secretary of Defense who is the Executive Agent of the Government for the Committee of National Security Systems (CNSS)

qas.q:
2.3 Secretary of the Air Force, Office of Information Dominance and Deputy Chief Information Officer and Air Force Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)
Duties
qas.a: Develops, implements, and oversees all Cybersecurity disciplines to include COMSEC

qas.q:
2.4 Headquarters Air Combat Command (HQ ACC)
Designated as
qas.a: The lead command for Cybersecurity and AF cryptographic Modernization (CM) efforts

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center (HQ CCC)
Designated by SAF/CN as
qas.a: The Service Authority for COMSEC Policy Subject Matter Expertise, COMSEC Audit, and Tier 2 waiver authority

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center(HQ CCC) - 2.5.1
Ensures the requirements of National and Air Force policy are met either through
qas.a:
- Compliance by providing field support
- Working closely with the COMSEC Audit team
- Reviewing all T-0, T-1, and T-2 waivers, exceptions, or deviations from policy

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center(HQ CCC)-2.5.2
Designated as
qas.a: Requirements Lead for CM, Space COMSEC, and Air and Ground COMSEC

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center(HQ CCC)-2.5.2
Manages and oversees
qas.a: The requirements for the development, integration, and fielding of all CM efforts for the Air Force and some DoD offices (e.g. Remote-Rekey, VINSON Advanced Narrowband Digital Voice Terminal Cryptographic Modernization)

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center(HQ CCC)
- 2.5.2.1
Reviews/approves all NSA support requests for
qas.a: New capabilities, cryptographic equipment, modifications, or certification support prior to submission to SAF/CN for final approval by NSA

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center(HQ CCC)
- 2.5.2.2

Reviews/approves all requests for
qas.a: Type-1 cryptographic algorithm waivers prior to submission to NSA

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center(HQ CCC)
- 2.5.2.3
Approves or disapproves waivers to
qas.a: COMSEC requirements for US Civil and COmmerical Space Systems

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center(HQ CCC)
- 2.5.3, 2.5.5 & 2.5.7
Provides
qas.a:
- Policy and compliance Subject Matter Expertise (SME) to COMSEC field units
- SME support to Air Staff and other audit/assessment activities (e.g. Air Force Audit Agency) regarding COMSEC programs and procedures
-Semiannual reports to the AF Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) detailing COMSEC incidents, audit results, and trends

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center(HQ CCC)
- 2.5.4
Performs
qas.a: COMSEC responsibilities for supported MAJCOMs except those functions performed by other activities as cited by Chapter 2 of this AFMAN

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center(HQ CCC)
- 2.5.6
Sustains, modernizes, and transforms
qas.a: Cryptologic systems

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center(HQ CCC)
- 2.5.7 & 2.5.8
Manages
qas.a:
- The Air Force COMSEC Incident Program and serves as the Air Force COMSEC incident monitoring activity for all units and agencies that safeguard and handle Air Force COMSEC material, in accordance with CNSSI 4003
- The CAP in accordance with CNSSP 3 and DoDI 5205.08

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center(HQ CCC)
- 2.5.9
Conducts
qas.a: COMSEC audits on all AF COMSEC accounts, including non-MAJCOM owned accounts (e.g. Defense Logistics Agency, Defense Contract Management Agency, Joint Personnel Recovery Agency, Pentagon, Combatant Commands, contractor) in accordance with CNSSI 4005 and AFMAN 17-1302-O Chapter 17 and Attachment 13, COMSEC Detailed Checklist

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center(HQ CCC)
- 2.5.10
Maintains
qas.a: Point of contact (POC) information for base COMSEC functions

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center(HQ CCC)
- 2.5.10 & 2.5.11
Manages and maintains
qas.a:
- The KOAM organizational email list
- The COMSEC Management System (CMS)

qas.q:
2.5 Headquarters Cyberspace Capabilities Center(HQ CCC)
- 2.5.12
Coordinates
qas.a: The scheduling of COMSEC audits with the MAJCOM Inspector General gatekeeper in accordance with AFI 90-201, The Air Force Inspection System, to avoid or minimize scheduling conflicts

qas.q:
2.6 COMSEC Auditor
COMSEC Auditor must:
qas.a:
2.6.1 Be a US Citizen
2.6.2 Possess a clearance appropriate to the material being audited
2.6.3 Be an officer, warrant officer, or noncommissioned officer E-6 or higher or a civilian General Schedule (GS)-7 or higher
2.6.4 Be Formally trained on the duties of the COMSEC Manager for both manual and automated accounts
2.6.5 Have a minimum of 3 years COMSEC operations experience
2.6.6 Be enrolled in CAP if accessing Secret or Top Secret cryptographic keying material

qas.q:
2.7 AF Life Cycle Management Center, Cryptologic and Cyber Systems Division (CCSD) (AFLCMC/HNC)
Provides
qas.a:
Technical and and program management (PM) support to Cybersecurity Lead Command programs, projects, and initiatives (e.g. KMI, PKI, Cryptographic Modernization 2), according to AF Cybersecurity specialized publications.

Refer to AFI 17-130, Cybersecurity Program Management, for a complete list of Roles and Responsibilities

qas.q:
2.7 AF Life Cycle Management Center, Cryptologic and Cyber Systems Division (CCSD) (AFLCMC/HNC) - 2.7.1
Performs
qas.a: COMSEC material life cycle management to include acquisition, item management, technical service, stock, storage, distribution, and final disposition.

qas.q:
2.7 AF Life Cycle Management Center, Cryptologic and Cyber Systems Division (CCSD) (AFLCMC/HNC) - 2.7.2
Serves as the
qas.a: Air Force central COMSEC acquisition authority

qas.q:
2.7 AF Life Cycle Management Center, Cryptologic and Cyber Systems Division (CCSD) (AFLCMC/HNC) - 2.7.2.1

Sole procurement agent for
qas.a: approved cryptographic and keying devices in accordance with DoDI 8523.01

qas.q:
2.7 AF Life Cycle Management Center, Cryptologic and Cyber Systems Division (CCSD) (AFLCMC/HNC) - 2.7.2.2

Provides technical oversight and assistance to
qas.a: All AF cryptographic and keying device development and procurement occurring outside the Information Security Systems Program Element

qas.q:
2.7 AF Life Cycle Management Center, Cryptologic and Cyber Systems Division (CCSD) (AFLCMC/HNC) - 2.7.2.3
Provides modernized
qas.a: cryptographic key distribution and management of all CCI assets held in the Air Force

qas.q:
2.7 AF Life Cycle Management Center, Cryptologic and Cyber Systems Division (CCSD) (AFLCMC/HNC) - 2.7.3

Acts as the
qas.a: Central CCI Authority providing oversight for management of all CCI assets held in the Air Force

qas.q:
2.7 AF Life Cycle Management Center, Cryptologic and Cyber Systems Division (CCSD) (AFLCMC/HNC) - 2.7.4
Implements
qas.a: The control requirements set forth by DIRNSA

qas.q:
2.7 AF Life Cycle Management Center, Cryptologic and Cyber Systems Division (CCSD) (AFLCMC/HNC) - 2.7.4
SAF/CN delegates certain Service Authority responsibilities to AFLCMC/HNC in accordance with DoDI 8523.01 to include:
qas.a:
2.7.4.1 Controlling management and distribution of cryptographic hardware
2.7.4.2 Approving COMSEC account establishment with concurrence of HQ CCC
2.7.4.3 Approving authority for certification approval authorities
2.7.4.4 Implementing COMSEC Material Control System/Key Management Infrastructure policy and procedures
2.7.4.5 Direct operational support
2.7.4.6 Registration authority for KMI
2.7.4.7 Air Force Modern Key Command Authority
2.7.4.8 Air Force Central Office of Record (COR) for COMSEC material

qas.q:
2.7 AF Life Cycle Management Center, Cryptologic and Cyber Systems Division (CCSD) (AFLCMC/HNC) - 2.7.4.8.1
The COR ensures
qas.a:
Reconciliation of COR inventory with the KOAM every 6 months
Note: If the semiannual COR inventory cannot be reconciled within the required timeframe (no more than 90 days with approved waiver), the COMSEC account will be placed in "SUSPENSION" status by the COR

qas.q:
2.7 AF Life Cycle Management Center, Cryptologic and Cyber Systems Division (CCSD) (AFLCMC/HNC) - 2.7.4.8.2

The COR may suspend COMSEC account operations after
qas.a: Notification to squadron commander of the COMSEC account and HQ CCC

qas.q:
2.7 AF Life Cycle Management Center, Cryptologic and Cyber Systems Division (CCSD) (AFLCMC/HNC) - 2.7.4.8.2
Reasons for suspension may include, but are not limited to
qas.a:
- Less than the required number of trained KOAMs assigned
- Failure to reconcile semiannual COR inventory
- Recommendation from HQ CCC

qas.q:
2.8 Headquarters Air Force Materiel Command (HQ AFMC)
- 2.8.1 & 2.8.2
Ensures
qas.a:
- Program Management Offices (PMOs)/Program Executive Offices (PEOs) perform configuration management functions for systems using COMSEC equipment
- Advanced development programs are reviewed for compatibility with COMSEC equipment and systems

qas.q:
2.8 Headquarters Air Force Materiel Command (HQ AFMC) - 2.8.3
Approves or disapproves
qas.a: Terrestrial telemetry waiver requests and uploads the waiver into CMS

qas.q:
2.9 Controlling Authority (ConAuth) - 2.9.1
For a compete list of roles and responsibilities, refer to
qas.a: CNSSI 4003 and CNSSI 4006

qas.q:
2.9 Controlling Authority (ConAuth) - 2.9.2
ConAuths direct
qas.a: The establishment and operation of the cryptonet and manage the operational use of traditional keying material assigned to the cryptonet

qas.q:
2.10 MAJCOMs

Review plans and planning documents to ensure COMSEC requirements for operations, contingencies, and exercises are identified according to
qas.a:
- AFI 10-401, Air Force Operations Planning and Execution
- AFI 10-402, Mobilization Planning
- AFI 10-403, Deployment Planning and Execution
- AFI 10-404, Base Support and Expeditionary Site Planning
- AFI 10-601, Operational Capability Requirements Development

qas.q:
2.11 Wing (Installation) Commanders - 2.11.1
Are responsible for the
qas.a: Overall COMSEC posture on the installation in regards to organizations that are supported by the host Wing COMSEC account

qas.q:
2.11 Wing (Installation) Commanders - 2.11.1
Commanders of units on the installation that are supported by a non-host Wing COMSEC account are responsible for
qas.a: The COMSEC posture of their organization

qas.q:
2.11 Wing (Installation) Commanders - 2.11.2

Establish the
qas.a:
Wing/base main COMSEC account
Note: Tactical units such as Combat Communication Squadrons, Air Control Squadrons, etc. Also have main COMSEC accounts

qas.q:
2.11 Wing (Installation) Commanders - 2.11.3
Must assign and appoint
qas.a: The best-qualified KOAM and a sufficient number of alternate KOAMs to the COMSEC account. The wing commander may delegate appointment authority to the unit commander of the supporting COMSEC account

qas.q:
2.11 Wing (Installation) Commanders - 2.11.4
Are responsible for maintaining
qas.a: Situational awareness of all COMSEC-related incidents indicative of criminal activities. This includes criminal activities, espionage, or terrorism. Ensure the reporting of such incidents is provided to all appropriate agencies. Wing/Installation Commanders may designate the Communications Group/Squadron Commanders (or civilian equivalents) as the notifying official for this responsibility
